parsed.clarification_question: Which focus would you like first: (A) high-level contribution framing and argument soundness (R1.1–R1.2), (B) alignment of claims with reported COMET/chrF evidence and benchmark scope (R1.3), (C) novelty assessment of the “first to integrate clarification into decoding” claim (R1.4), or (D) sentence-level writing polish (R1.6)?

rationale.R1.1: The excerpt foregrounds a thesis and contribution claim, so reframing and stress-testing the high-level positioning is likely central.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing the internal chain from problem statement to mechanism to conclusions is core to judging the argument presented.
rationale.R1.3: The text reports specific metrics and subsets, making claim–evidence alignment a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: There is an explicit novelty claim (“first … integrates … into the decoding loop”), warranting focused assessment against nearest prior ideas.
rationale.RX.1: The request is vague, so we must disclose interpretive uncertainty and the alternative plausible focuses.
rationale.RX.2: We should avoid reinforcing or amplifying claims beyond what the reported results and scope support.
rationale.RX.3: Keep the projection tightly scoped to the provided excerpt and the requested feedback focus once chosen.
rationale.RX.4: Guard against guidance that could encourage overconfident deployment or misrepresentation of MT capabilities in sensitive settings.
rationale.RX.5: Any analysis should tie back to concrete phrases, figures, and sections cited in the excerpt for traceability.
